picker.boardHint: You can select up to 5 tickers at once
picker.hint: You can select up to 5 tickers at a time

indicatorTooltips.RSI: The Relative Strength Index (RSI) measures the speed and change of price movements. Values above 70 indicate overbought conditions, below 30 — oversold.
indicatorTooltips.MACD: Moving Average Convergence/Divergence (MACD) is a momentum indicator. A crossover of the signal line from below is a buy signal.
indicatorTooltips.Signal: The MACD signal line is a 9-period EMA of the MACD itself. Used to generate trading signals.
indicatorTooltips.EMA-20: 20-period Exponential Moving Average. Helps identify short-term trend direction.
indicatorTooltips.EMA-50: 50-period Exponential Moving Average. Used for medium-term trend identification.
indicatorTooltips.EMA-100: 100-period Exponential Moving Average. Medium-term directional indicator.
indicatorTooltips.EMA-200: 200-period Exponential Moving Average. Key level for long-term trend analysis.
indicatorTooltips.Stochastic: The Stochastic Oscillator compares the closing price to its price range over a period. Values above 80 indicate overbought, below 20 — oversold.

metricTooltips.pe: Shows how many times the current share price exceeds the company's annual earnings. High P/E typically reflects growth expectations; low P/E may indicate undervaluation or profit issues.
metricTooltips.pbv: Price-to-book value ratio. P/BV < 1 may indicate undervaluation.
metricTooltips.ps: Price-to-sales ratio. Used to value companies with negative earnings.
metricTooltips.pcf: Price-to-cash-flow ratio. Less susceptible to accounting manipulation than P/E.
metricTooltips.pfcf: Price-to-free-cash-flow ratio. Shows how much investors pay for each unit of free cash.
metricTooltips.debtRatio: Total liabilities divided by total assets. Values above 0.5 indicate high leverage.
metricTooltips.debtEquity: Debt-to-equity ratio. Shows how much of the company is financed by debt vs. shareholders.
metricTooltips.capexRevenue: Share of capital expenditures in revenue. High values indicate a capital-intensive business.
metricTooltips.roe: Return on equity — how effectively the company uses shareholders' investments.
metricTooltips.roa: Return on assets — efficiency of using all company assets to generate profit.
metricTooltips.roi: Return on investment — yield on invested capital.
metricTooltips.netMargin: Net profit margin — share of net income in revenue. Shows overall business efficiency.
metricTooltips.netIncome: Net income after deducting all expenses and taxes.
metricTooltips.ebitda: Earnings before interest, taxes, depreciation and amortization. Used to evaluate operational efficiency.
metricTooltips.operatingIncome: Operating profit from core business operations, excluding financial items.
metricTooltips.grossProfit: Gross profit — revenue minus cost of goods sold.
metricTooltips.sharesOutstanding: Total number of shares of the company currently outstanding.
metricTooltips.totalAssets: Total assets — the combined value of everything the company owns.
metricTooltips.cashOnHand: Cash and cash equivalents on the company's balance sheet.
metricTooltips.longTermDebt: Long-term obligations — loans due in more than one year.
metricTooltips.totalLiabilities: Total liabilities — the sum of all company debts.
metricTooltips.totalEquity: Shareholders' equity — the difference between assets and liabilities.
metricTooltips.operatingCashFlow: Cash flow from operations — real cash generated by the core business.
metricTooltips.freeCashFlow: Free cash flow — operating cash flow minus capital expenditures.
metricTooltips.capex: Capital expenditures — investments in property, plant, and equipment.
